---
title: "Data Cleaning"
subtitle: ""
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior 

categories: []
tags: [""]
title-block-banner: false
format: 
  html: 
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em

jupyter: python3

---


```{python}
import pandas as pd

import importlib
import src.data_analysis.feature_discrimination_plots as utils

importlib.reload(utils)
from src.data_analysis.feature_discrimination_plots import plot_continuous_vs_categorical, contingency_analysis, plot_grouped_bar

```

```{python}
# Impoort data from datasets
path_datasets = "/Users/juniorjumbong/Desktop/personal-website/00_tds/01_PD_credit_scoring/datasets"
```

```{python}
# Import data from datasets
train_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/train_imputed.csv")
test_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/test_imputed.csv")
oot_imputed = pd.read_csv(f"{path_datasets}/oot_imputed.csv")
```





```{python}
plot_continuous_vs_categorical(
    df=train_imputed,
    continuous_var="person_income",
    categorical_var="def",
    category_labels={0: "No Default", 1: "Default"},
    figsize=(14, 12),
    sample=5000
)
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency


```

```{python}
results = contingency_analysis(
     df=train_imputed,
    var1="def",
    var2="person_home_ownership",
    normalize=None   # très utile en scoring
)
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency

# ── 1. Tableau de contingence ────────────────────────────────────────────────
contingency = pd.crosstab(
    train_imputed["person_home_ownership"],
    train_imputed["def"],
    margins=True
)
print("=== Tableau de contingence (effectifs) ===")
print(contingency)

# ── 2. Tableau des fréquences relatives (normalisé par ligne) ────────────────
contingency_norm = pd.crosstab(
    train_imputed["person_home_ownership"],
    train_imputed["def"],
    normalize="index"
).round(3) * 100
print("\n=== Taux de défaut par modalité (%) ===")
print(contingency_norm)

# ── 3. Test du Chi² ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ct = pd.crosstab(train_imputed["person_home_ownership"], train_imputed["def"])
chi2, p_value, dof, expected = chi2_contingency(ct)

n = ct.values.sum()
cramers_v = np.sqrt(chi2 / (n * (min(ct.shape) - 1)))

print(f"\n=== Test du Chi² ===")
print(f"Chi²      : {chi2:.4f}")
print(f"p-value   : {p_value:.4f}")
print(f"Degrés lib: {dof}")
print(f"V de Cramér: {cramers_v:.4f}")

if p_value < 0.05:
    print("→ Liaison statistiquement significative (p < 0.05)")
else:
    print("→ Pas de liaison significative (p ≥ 0.05)")

# ── 4. Interprétation du V de Cramér ────────────────────────────────────────
if cramers_v < 0.10:
    strength = "très faible"
elif cramers_v < 0.20:
    strength = "faible"
elif cramers_v < 0.30:
    strength = "modérée"
else:
    strength = "forte"
print(f"→ Force de la liaison : {strength} (V = {cramers_v:.3f})")

# ── 5. Visualisation (principes de Cole) ────────────────────────────────────


plot_grouped_bar(
    df=train_imputed,
    cat_var="def",
    subcat_var="person_home_ownership",
    normalize="index",
    title="Default Rate by Home Ownership"
)
```


```{python}
print(train_imputed.dtypes)
continuous_vars = [
    "person_income",
    "person_age",
    "person_emp_length",
    "loan_amnt",
    "loan_int_rate",
    "loan_percent_income",
    "cb_person_cred_hist_length"
]

qualitative_vars = [
    "person_home_ownership",
    "cb_person_default_on_file",
    "loan_intent",
    "loan_grade",
]
```
